principles.label: Organic Growth Over External Funding
principles.summary: Avoid rushing to raise venture capital or go public; a lack of external funding forces operational discipline and allows founders to retain control.
principles.details: Being unable to raise venture capital or bank credit forces a company to grow organically. This instills a deep operational discipline that cannot be easily replicated, prevents founders from losing control of their business, and avoids the negative pressures of running a public company.
principles.representative_quote: because we weren't able to raise money all of our growth over the last twenty nine years had to be organic and that gives a company a certain amount of discipline that cannot easily be really covered

principles.label: Egalitarian Corporate Culture
principles.summary: Eliminate executive perks to ensure every employee feels valued and respected.
principles.details: Creating an egalitarian culture where employees feel respected translates directly into those employees providing exceptional, loyal service to customers. True power and business strength come from staying grounded and treating employees with fundamental respect.
principles.representative_quote: We have no executive parking... We don't have a special executive compensation plan. We try to make sure that everybody feels valued.

principles.label: Conversations Over Targeting
principles.summary: Engage in genuine conversations with customers to understand their needs rather than treating them as sales targets.
principles.details: By talking to customers, you can truly understand their current state and future goals, which is necessary to help them select, deploy, and manage the right technology.
principles.representative_quote: We don't 'target' customers, we talk to them. We understand where they are and where they need to go.

principles.label: Long-Term Focus on Controllables
principles.summary: Direct all your energy toward actionable steps and long-term planning rather than stressing over external factors or insecurities.
principles.details: Even if you start at a disadvantage or feel inadequate, an accumulation of directed energy, focus, and effort over decades compounds into powerful results. Stop beating yourself up over things you cannot control.
principles.representative_quote: if you focus if you know what you want and you put together a long-term plan you can catch up in life no matter where you are

principles.label: Practical Technology Adoption
principles.summary: Technology must provide more utility than the effort required to learn it.
principles.details: Adopting new gadgets or technology just to be an early adopter is inefficient; the practical value extracted must outweigh the learning curve.
principles.representative_quote: Gadgets have to provide real utility. I have to extract more out of it than I would put into learning about it.

principles.label: Acceptance of Failure
principles.summary: The fear of failure shouldn't stop you from pursuing ambitious goals.
principles.details: Even if a venture does not succeed, the attempt itself is valuable because you will know you gave it your best effort.
principles.representative_quote: it is okay to fail at least you will have tried and you will have done your best

principles.label: Corporate Social Responsibility
principles.summary: Organizations should use their collective voice to support peaceful protests against inequality and human rights violations.
principles.details: As a corporate leader, it is important to publicly stand against injustice and inequality alongside your employees.
principles.representative_quote: Today the employees of SHI lend our voices in support of the peaceful protestors of the most recent episodes of inequality, injustice, and human rights violations.

principles.label: Evolving Brand Identity
principles.summary: A company's brand identity must reflect both its current capabilities and its future trajectory.
principles.details: As a business evolves from a traditional model into a solutions provider, its public identity needs to update to match its new reality and its ongoing commitment to innovation.
principles.representative_quote: An identity that is both fit for the technology solutions provider we have become and the innovator we will continue to be in the future.

frameworks.label: 100-Year Decade-by-Decade Planning
frameworks.summary: A method for crafting long-term life and career goals by dividing a 100-year lifespan into distinct, singularly focused decades.
frameworks.details: 1. Assume you will live to be 100 years old to establish a long-term horizon. 2. Create a template for each decade of your life. 3. Assign a singular, overarching focus to each decade (e.g., 20s for learning/preparation, 30s for extreme startup focus, 40s for family integration). 4. Score yourself regularly on whether you are accomplishing the specific goals within your current decade.
frameworks.representative_quote: actually assumed first of all that I would live to a hundred and actually put together a template for each decade

frameworks.label: Holistic SMART Goals
frameworks.summary: A framework for setting specific objectives within your life plan across health, family, and career.
frameworks.details: 1. Divide your life into three holistic aspects: personal/family, career/financial, and health. 2. Make goals Specific, Measurable, Actionable, Relatable, and Time-bound. 3. Ensure health goals are prioritized first, as they are the foundation for the other two aspects.
frameworks.representative_quote: setting goals you need to make it smart so specific measurable actionable relatable time-bound

frameworks.label: Career Selection by Elimination
frameworks.summary: A method for determining a career path by systematically crossing out professions where personal limitations would prevent top-tier success.
frameworks.details: 1. Identify personal limitations or weaknesses (e.g., language barriers). 2. Evaluate potential professions and cross out any where those limitations would prevent top-tier success. 3. Commit fully to the remaining viable options.
frameworks.representative_quote: I knew then that the best chance of success for me was to start my own business, because after I x-ed out all the professions I could not be successful in, that's what I was left with.

mental_models.label: Salesperson as Client CEO
mental_models.summary: Viewing each salesperson not as a vendor pushing products, but as the chief executive or president of their client's success.
mental_models.details: This instills absolute ownership and empowerment over customer relationships, allowing representatives to make their own decisions on how to manage and serve their customers.

mental_models.label: Disadvantage as Fuel
mental_models.summary: Reframing personal insecurities or lack of cultural familiarity not as inhibitors, but as the primary source of motivation to out-prepare and out-work others.
mental_models.details: For example, using insecurity about language skills and unfamiliarity with American culture to craft the most ambitious goals possible and save up for business school.
mental_models.representative_quote: i think low self-esteem was the source of motivation for me to work harder it's an accumulation of directed energy focus and effort and over decades that can be very powerful

mental_models.label: The Biological Timetable
mental_models.summary: Viewing family planning as a fixed biological constraint that must be strategically factored into long-term career sequencing, rather than left to chance.
mental_models.details: She dedicated her 30s entirely to her startup to avoid distractions, but explicitly planned to start a family at 39 because she 'knew about the fertility curve' and the set timetable for securing a family.

mental_models.label: Health as the Ultimate Prerequisite
mental_models.summary: Treating physical health not as a separate goal, but as the foundational prerequisite that enables all career and financial goals to exist.
mental_models.details: Setting a specific goal to live beyond 100 by tracking BMI, weight, and cholesterol annually, reasoning that without health, nothing else matters.
mental_models.representative_quote: Without your health there's really nothing else right that matters in life.

mental_models.label: Right Livelihood
mental_models.summary: A guiding philosophy for leadership and business that emphasizes staying grounded, human, and respectful, rather than succumbing to the traditional trappings of executive power.
mental_models.details: Choosing to book her own travel and answer her own phone despite running a multi-billion dollar tech empire.
mental_models.representative_quote: a company is only as strong as the respect it shows its people.

heuristics.label: Delay Outside Funding
heuristics.summary: Delay going public and raising outside money as long as possible.
heuristics.representative_quote: Don't be so eager to go and raise money and give all your shares away.

heuristics.label: Focus on Controllables
heuristics.summary: Only focus on things that you have control over.
heuristics.representative_quote: don't beat yourself up only focus on things that you have control over don't worry about it rest of it

heuristics.label: Begin With the End in Mind
heuristics.summary: Begin with the end in mind when planning your life and career.

heuristics.label: Absolute Customer Ownership
heuristics.summary: If you are responsible for a customer, you own that relationship entirely.

heuristics.label: Utility Over Novelty
heuristics.summary: Gadgets have to provide real utility to be worth adopting.
heuristics.representative_quote: Gadgets have to provide real utility. I have to extract more out of it than I would put into learning about it.

heuristics.label: Stay Grounded
heuristics.summary: Book your own travel and answer your own phone to stay grounded.

signature_quotes.label: On Overcoming Disadvantages
signature_quotes.summary: A quote on how long-term planning and focus can overcome early disadvantages.
signature_quotes.details: Reflecting on the true source of her success and overcoming feelings of inadequacy.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: if you focus if you know what you want and you put together a long-term plan you can catch up in life no matter where you are

signature_quotes.label: On Customer Engagement
signature_quotes.summary: A quote describing SHI's core approach to customer service and technology consulting.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: We don't 'target' customers, we talk to them. We understand where they are and where they need to go. Then we help them select, deploy, and manage the technology that will get them there.

signature_quotes.label: On Egalitarian Culture
signature_quotes.summary: A quote describing the egalitarian culture she built at SHI.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: We have no executive parking... We don't have a special executive compensation plan. We try to make sure that everybody feels valued.

signature_quotes.label: On Choosing Entrepreneurship
signature_quotes.summary: A quote discussing how she chose entrepreneurship after realizing her weaknesses might hold her back in other fields.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: I knew then that the best chance of success for me was to start my own business, because after I x-ed out all the professions I could not be successful in, that's what I was left with.

signature_quotes.label: On the Value of Health
signature_quotes.summary: A quote explaining why health goals are the foundation of her holistic life plan.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Without your health there's really nothing else right that matters in life.

signature_quotes.label: On the Blessing of No Funding
signature_quotes.summary: A quote encouraging entrepreneurs who are struggling to find external investors.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: If you don't [raise money] it's okay because maybe like myself you would be grateful for not being able to raise money 30 years later.

signature_quotes.label: On the Source of Motivation
signature_quotes.summary: A quote on using low self-esteem as fuel for hard work.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: i'm not really extraordinary... i've been very lucky and i've been well prepared i think low self-esteem was the source of motivation for me to work harder it's an accumulation of directed energy focus and effort and over decades that can be very powerful

signature_quotes.label: On Accepting Failure
signature_quotes.summary: A quote offering closing advice to entrepreneurs embarking on scary journeys.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: It is okay to fail at least you will have tried and you will have done your best.

anti_patterns.label: Rushing to Raise VC or IPO
anti_patterns.summary: Rushing to take a company public or taking outside investment too early.
anti_patterns.details: If you take outside investors, you lose control over the company and many other aspects of your business, and are forced to deal with the negatives of running a public entity. It compromises strategic autonomy and long-term vision.

anti_patterns.label: Targeting Customers
anti_patterns.summary: Treating customers as mere sales prospects or 'targets' to acquire.
anti_patterns.details: It fails to uncover where the customer currently is and where they need to go, which is essential for providing effective technology solutions.

anti_patterns.label: Executive Perks
anti_patterns.summary: Implementing executive tiers with special perks like reserved parking or exclusive compensation plans.
anti_patterns.details: It damages the egalitarian culture and prevents employees from feeling valued, which ultimately hurts their motivation to serve customers.

anti_patterns.label: Tech for Tech's Sake
anti_patterns.summary: Adopting technology just to be an early adopter.
anti_patterns.details: The time and effort put into learning the new technology often outweighs the actual utility extracted from it.

anti_patterns.label: Letting Insecurities Inhibit Ambition
anti_patterns.summary: Allowing personal insecurities or lack of familiarity to prevent you from setting high goals.
anti_patterns.details: It prevents you from crafting ambitious goals; instead, these weaknesses should be used as motivation to prepare harder.
